concepts.label: Algorithm Pattern Frequency
concepts.description: The empirical fact that 15–20 algorithm patterns cover ~80% of FAANG interview problems.

concepts.label: Interview Pareto Principle
concepts.description: The 80/20 rule applied to interview prep: a small set of high-frequency patterns yields disproportionate coverage.

concepts.label: Empirically Validated Study Sequence
concepts.description: The dependency-ordered learning path (Arrays → Hashing → Trees → Graphs → DP) derived from NeetCode, Blind 75, and Grind 75.

concepts.label: Study Dependency DAG
concepts.description: The directed acyclic graph of algorithm topic dependencies that determines the optimal study order.

concepts.label: Interview Communication
concepts.description: The practice of narrating your problem-solving process clearly during a coding interview.

concepts.label: UMPIRE Framework
concepts.description: The six-step interview scaffold: Understand, Match, Plan, Implement, Review, Evaluate.

concepts.label: Hash Map
concepts.description: Key-value store with O(1) average lookup, insertion, and deletion based on hash functions.

concepts.label: Hash Set
concepts.description: Unordered collection with O(1) average membership test, insertion, and deletion.

concepts.label: Space-Time Tradeoff
concepts.description: The principle of using extra memory (a hash map or set) to reduce time complexity.

concepts.label: Frequency Counting Pattern
concepts.description: Using a hash map to count occurrences of elements for anagram detection, sliding window, and Top-K problems.

concepts.label: Complement Lookup Pattern
concepts.description: Two Sum style: store seen values; for each element check if (target - element) exists in the map.

concepts.label: Index-as-Key Pattern
concepts.description: Using array indices as hash map keys when values are bounded integers (e.g. first missing positive).

concepts.label: Language Hidden Complexity Costs
concepts.description: Operations that appear O(1) but are O(n) or worse in specific language implementations.

concepts.label: Two-Pointer Pattern
concepts.description: Left-right converging or fast-slow pointer techniques for pair search, cycle detection, and partition.

concepts.label: Sliding Window Pattern
concepts.description: Expand-right / shrink-left window for fixed-size and variable-size subarray/substring optimisation.

concepts.label: Prefix Sum
concepts.description: Precomputed cumulative sum array enabling O(1) range sum queries after O(n) setup.

concepts.label: Sort Preprocessing
concepts.description: Sorting first in O(n log n) to enable O(n) two-pointer or O(log n) binary search, beating O(n²) brute force.

concepts.label: Tree Traversal
concepts.description: Pre-order, in-order, post-order, and level-order traversal templates for binary trees.

concepts.label: BST Invariant
concepts.description: All left descendants < node ≤ all right descendants; enables in-order sorted output and O(log n) lookup in balanced BSTs.

concepts.label: BFS (Breadth-First Search)
concepts.description: Level-by-level traversal using a deque; guarantees shortest path in unweighted graphs; O(V+E).

concepts.label: DFS (Depth-First Search)
concepts.description: Recursive or iterative depth-first traversal for path existence, cycle detection, and topological sort; O(V+E).

concepts.label: Cycle Detection
concepts.description: DFS three-colour marking (white/gray/black) to detect directed cycles; Floyd's fast-slow for linked-list cycles.

concepts.label: Topological Sort
concepts.description: Linear ordering of DAG nodes where each node precedes its dependents; Kahn's BFS or DFS post-order reversal.

concepts.label: Binary Search
concepts.description: Unified `while lo < hi` template for finding leftmost or rightmost True in a monotone predicate; O(log n).

concepts.label: Heap (Priority Queue)
concepts.description: Complete binary tree satisfying the heap property; O(log n) push/pop, O(n) heapify, O(1) peek.

concepts.label: Top-K Heap Pattern
concepts.description: Use a min-heap of size K to find the K largest elements in O(n log K); root is the Kth largest.

concepts.label: Monotonic Stack
concepts.description: Stack maintained in sorted order; while-violated-pop-then-push loop for Next Greater Element, histogram, and rain water in O(n) amortised.

concepts.label: Big-O Notation
concepts.description: Formal asymptotic upper bound: f(n) = O(g(n)) iff ∃ c, n₀ such that f(n) ≤ c·g(n) for all n ≥ n₀.

concepts.label: Complexity Growth Hierarchy
concepts.description: O(1) < O(log n) < O(n) < O(n log n) < O(n²) < O(2ⁿ) < O(n!) — the canonical ordering for interview complexity comparisons.

concepts.label: Master Theorem
concepts.description: Three-case formula for solving divide-and-conquer recurrences T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n).

concepts.label: Divide and Conquer
concepts.description: Problem decomposition into independent subproblems of size n/b, solved recursively, then combined.

concepts.label: Amortised Analysis
concepts.description: Aggregate method: average per-operation cost over a sequence of n operations; table doubling proof.

concepts.label: Union-Find (DSU)
concepts.description: Disjoint set union with path compression and union by rank; O(α(n)) ≈ O(1) amortised per operation.

concepts.label: Recursion Space Complexity
concepts.description: Call stack depth equals recursion space: O(log n) for balanced trees, O(n) for skewed trees and linear recursion.

concepts.label: Python Recursion Limit
concepts.description: Python's default 1000-frame limit and OS stack limit; iterative stack as the robust workaround.

concepts.label: Dynamic Programming
concepts.description: Optimisation technique for problems with optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems; brute-force → memoised → tabulated pipeline.

concepts.label: Memoisation (Top-Down DP)
concepts.description: Caching recursive call results with @functools.cache; converts O(2^n) to O(n) for Fibonacci-style problems.

concepts.label: Tabulation (Bottom-Up DP)
concepts.description: Iterative DP filling a table from base cases; eliminates call-stack overhead and enables space optimisation.

concepts.label: DP Space Optimisation
concepts.description: Reducing O(n²) DP table to O(n) or O(1) by keeping only the rows/columns the recurrence depends on.

concepts.label: DP State Design
concepts.description: Defining the minimum state that captures all information needed for optimal forward decisions (Markov property).

concepts.label: Python Hidden O(n) Operations
concepts.description: list.pop(0), list.insert(0,x), string +=, x in list — operations that appear O(1) but are O(n) in Python.

concepts.label: String Concatenation O(n²) Trap
concepts.description: Immutable strings mean result += char creates a new copy on every iteration; total cost O(n²). Fix: join() or StringBuilder.

concepts.label: Backtracking
concepts.description: Systematic search via choose–explore–unchoose; builds a search tree with pruning to eliminate invalid branches early.
